title: Kangaroo Level 1-2 - Practice Set 6: Counting & Logic
disclaimer: Original practice problems inspired by Math Kangaroo format. Not official AKSF content.

questions.question_text: How many red apples are shown on the table below?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 300 120' font-family='sans-serif'><rect x='10' y='80' width='280' height='20' fill='#e8e8e8' stroke='currentColor'/><circle cx='40' cy='60' r='15' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/><circle cx='90' cy='60' r='15' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/><circle cx='140' cy='60' r='15' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/><circle cx='190' cy='60' r='15' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/><circle cx='240' cy='60' r='15' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/></svg>
questions.solution: Count the circles on the table one by one: 1, 2, 3, 4, 5. There are 5 apples.

questions.question_text: How many triangles make up the small house drawing?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 200 180' font-family='sans-serif'><rect x='50' y='90' width='100' height='70' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/><polygon points='50,90 150,90 100,40' fill='#e8e8e8' stroke='currentColor'/></svg>
questions.solution: Only the roof is a triangle. The rest of the house is a rectangle. So there is 1 triangle.

questions.question_text: Which shape comes next in the sequence: circle, square, circle, square, circle, ?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 300 60' font-family='sans-serif'><circle cx='25' cy='30' r='15' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/><rect x='55' y='15' width='30' height='30' fill='#e8e8e8' stroke='currentColor'/><circle cx='110' cy='30' r='15' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/><rect x='140' y='15' width='30' height='30' fill='#e8e8e8' stroke='currentColor'/><circle cx='195' cy='30' r='15' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/><text x='240' y='38' font-size='28'>?</text></svg>
questions.solution: The pattern alternates circle and square. After a circle comes a square.

questions.question_text: Mia has 3 balloons. Her brother gives her 2 more balloons. How many balloons does Mia have now?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 260 140' font-family='sans-serif'><circle cx='30' cy='45' r='16' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/><line x1='30' y1='61' x2='30' y2='115' stroke='currentColor'/><circle cx='75' cy='45' r='16' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/><line x1='75' y1='61' x2='75' y2='115' stroke='currentColor'/><circle cx='120' cy='45' r='16' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/><line x1='120' y1='61' x2='120' y2='115' stroke='currentColor'/><text x='150' y='55' font-size='22'>+</text><circle cx='195' cy='45' r='16' fill='#e8e8e8' stroke='currentColor'/><line x1='195' y1='61' x2='195' y2='115' stroke='currentColor'/><circle cx='240' cy='45' r='16' fill='#e8e8e8' stroke='currentColor'/><line x1='240' y1='61' x2='240' y2='115' stroke='currentColor'/></svg>
questions.image_description: Three balloons plus two more balloons on strings
questions.solution: Adding 3 balloons and 2 balloons gives 3 + 2 = 5 balloons.

questions.question_text: How many stars are inside the rectangle frame?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 260 120' font-family='sans-serif'><rect x='10' y='10' width='240' height='100' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/><text x='40' y='70' font-size='28'>*</text><text x='80' y='70' font-size='28'>*</text><text x='120' y='70' font-size='28'>*</text><text x='160' y='70' font-size='28'>*</text><text x='200' y='70' font-size='28'>*</text><text x='240' y='70' font-size='28'>*</text></svg>
questions.solution: Count each star inside the rectangle: 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6. There are 6 stars.

questions.question_text: Which shape in the row has exactly four equal sides?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 300 80' font-family='sans-serif'><circle cx='30' cy='40' r='20' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/><polygon points='90,60 120,60 105,20' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/><rect x='150' y='20' width='40' height='40' fill='#e8e8e8' stroke='currentColor'/><rect x='210' y='25' width='60' height='30' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/></svg>
questions.solution: A square has four sides all the same length. The rectangle shown is longer than it is tall.

questions.question_text: Three kids line up: Ana, Ben, Cat. Ben is behind Ana. Cat is behind Ben. Who is last?
questions.solution: The order from front to back is Ana, Ben, Cat. Cat is last.

questions.question_text: How many sides does the stop-sign shape (octagon) below have?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 120 120' font-family='sans-serif'><polygon points='40,10 80,10 110,40 110,80 80,110 40,110 10,80 10,40' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/></svg>
questions.solution: An octagon, shaped like a stop sign, has 8 sides.

questions.question_text: How many small squares are inside the 3 by 3 grid shown?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 150 150' font-family='sans-serif'><rect x='10' y='10' width='40' height='40' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/><rect x='55' y='10' width='40' height='40' fill='#e8e8e8' stroke='currentColor'/><rect x='100' y='10' width='40' height='40' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/><rect x='10' y='55' width='40' height='40' fill='#e8e8e8' stroke='currentColor'/><rect x='55' y='55' width='40' height='40' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/><rect x='100' y='55' width='40' height='40' fill='#e8e8e8' stroke='currentColor'/><rect x='10' y='100' width='40' height='40' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/><rect x='55' y='100' width='40' height='40' fill='#e8e8e8' stroke='currentColor'/><rect x='100' y='100' width='40' height='40' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/></svg>
questions.solution: A 3 by 3 grid has 3 rows of 3 small squares: 3 + 3 + 3 = 9.

questions.question_text: Tom picked 7 flowers. He gave 3 to his mother. How many flowers does Tom still have?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 300 120' font-family='sans-serif'><circle cx='25' cy='35' r='10' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/><line x1='25' y1='45' x2='25' y2='95' stroke='currentColor'/><circle cx='60' cy='35' r='10' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/><line x1='60' y1='45' x2='60' y2='95' stroke='currentColor'/><circle cx='95' cy='35' r='10' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/><line x1='95' y1='45' x2='95' y2='95' stroke='currentColor'/><circle cx='130' cy='35' r='10' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/><line x1='130' y1='45' x2='130' y2='95' stroke='currentColor'/><circle cx='180' cy='35' r='10' fill='#e8e8e8' stroke='currentColor' stroke-dasharray='3,2'/><line x1='180' y1='45' x2='180' y2='95' stroke='currentColor' stroke-dasharray='3,2'/><circle cx='215' cy='35' r='10' fill='#e8e8e8' stroke='currentColor' stroke-dasharray='3,2'/><line x1='215' y1='45' x2='215' y2='95' stroke='currentColor' stroke-dasharray='3,2'/><circle cx='250' cy='35' r='10' fill='#e8e8e8' stroke='currentColor' stroke-dasharray='3,2'/><line x1='250' y1='45' x2='250' y2='95' stroke='currentColor' stroke-dasharray='3,2'/></svg>
questions.image_description: Seven flowers: four remaining and three (dashed) given away
questions.solution: Subtract the flowers given away: 7 - 3 = 4 flowers remain with Tom.

questions.question_text: Four kangaroos sit in seats 1, 2, 3, 4. Kiki is not in 1 or 4. Kiki is between Lulu and Mimi. Mimi is in seat 4. Which seat is Kiki in?
questions.solution: Mimi is in 4 and Kiki is between Lulu and Mimi, so Kiki sits in 3 and Lulu in 2.

questions.question_text: How many circles are completely inside the big square shown?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 200 200' font-family='sans-serif'><rect x='20' y='20' width='160' height='160' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/><circle cx='55' cy='55' r='15' fill='#e8e8e8' stroke='currentColor'/><circle cx='145' cy='55' r='15' fill='#e8e8e8' stroke='currentColor'/><circle cx='100' cy='100' r='15' fill='#e8e8e8' stroke='currentColor'/><circle cx='55' cy='145' r='15' fill='#e8e8e8' stroke='currentColor'/><circle cx='190' cy='145' r='15' fill='#e8e8e8' stroke='currentColor'/></svg>
questions.solution: Four circles are fully inside the square. The right-bottom circle sticks out, so it does not count.

questions.question_text: A kangaroo hops from 10 to 16 by jumping 2 steps each time. How many hops does it take?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 300 80' font-family='sans-serif'><line x1='20' y1='50' x2='280' y2='50' stroke='currentColor'/><circle cx='30' cy='50' r='4' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/><text x='22' y='70' font-size='12'>10</text><circle cx='80' cy='50' r='4' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/><text x='72' y='70' font-size='12'>12</text><circle cx='130' cy='50' r='4' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/><text x='122' y='70' font-size='12'>14</text><circle cx='180' cy='50' r='4' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/><text x='172' y='70' font-size='12'>16</text></svg>
questions.solution: From 10 to 12 is 1 hop, 12 to 14 is 2 hops, 14 to 16 is 3 hops.

questions.question_text: Which letter below has exactly one straight line of symmetry?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 260 80' font-family='sans-serif'><text x='20' y='55' font-size='40'>A</text><text x='70' y='55' font-size='40'>F</text><text x='120' y='55' font-size='40'>H</text><text x='170' y='55' font-size='40'>P</text><text x='220' y='55' font-size='40'>Z</text></svg>
questions.solution: Letter A has one vertical line of symmetry. F, P, and Z have none. H has two lines of symmetry.

questions.question_text: Sam has 4 red marbles and twice as many blue marbles. How many marbles does Sam have in total?
questions.solution: Blue marbles: 2 times 4 equals 8. Total: 4 + 8 = 12 marbles.

questions.question_text: A box holds only apples and pears. There are 9 fruits total. If 5 are apples, how many pears are in the box?
questions.solution: Pears equal total minus apples: 9 - 5 = 4 pears.

questions.question_text: How many triangles can you count in the figure made by a square with both of its diagonals drawn?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 150 150' font-family='sans-serif'><rect x='20' y='20' width='110' height='110' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/><line x1='20' y1='20' x2='130' y2='130' stroke='currentColor'/><line x1='130' y1='20' x2='20' y2='130' stroke='currentColor'/></svg>
questions.solution: The diagonals make 4 small triangles plus 4 larger triangles (each taking up half the square), giving 8.

questions.question_text: In a row of 5 houses, the red house is first. The blue house is next to the red house. The green house is last. Which house is in the middle if yellow is next to green?
questions.solution: Positions: red(1), blue(2), green(5), yellow(4). Position 3 must be the remaining color, white.

questions.question_text: The figure shows an L-shape made of 5 equal squares. How many sides does its outline have?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 180 180' font-family='sans-serif'><polygon points='20,20 60,20 60,100 140,100 140,140 20,140' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/></svg>
questions.solution: Trace the L-outline: 6 straight edges form the boundary.

questions.question_text: Lara reads 5 pages on Monday, 6 pages on Tuesday, and 7 pages on Wednesday. How many pages does she read in these three days?
questions.solution: Add the pages: 5 + 6 + 7 = 18 pages.

questions.question_text: How many small cubes are used to build the staircase made of 3 steps shown below?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 180 150' font-family='sans-serif'><rect x='10' y='110' width='30' height='30' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/><rect x='40' y='110' width='30' height='30' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/><rect x='40' y='80' width='30' height='30' fill='#e8e8e8' stroke='currentColor'/><rect x='70' y='110' width='30' height='30' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/><rect x='70' y='80' width='30' height='30' fill='#e8e8e8' stroke='currentColor'/><rect x='70' y='50' width='30' height='30' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/></svg>
questions.solution: Step 1: 1 cube, Step 2: 2 cubes, Step 3: 3 cubes. Total: 1 + 2 + 3 = 6 cubes.

questions.question_text: Five kangaroos share 10 carrots equally. Two kangaroos trade their share with each other. How many carrots does each kangaroo now have?
questions.solution: 10 divided by 5 equals 2 carrots each. Trading equal shares does not change the count.

questions.question_text: A pencil costs 4 coins and an eraser costs 2 coins. Nina buys 2 pencils and 1 eraser. How many coins does she spend?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 280 150' font-family='sans-serif'><rect x='20' y='35' width='70' height='14' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/><polygon points='90,35 105,42 90,49' fill='#e8e8e8' stroke='currentColor'/><text x='30' y='68' font-size='12'>4 coins</text><rect x='20' y='85' width='70' height='14' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/><polygon points='90,85 105,92 90,99' fill='#e8e8e8' stroke='currentColor'/><text x='30' y='118' font-size='12'>4 coins</text><rect x='180' y='60' width='55' height='25' rx='4' fill='#e8e8e8' stroke='currentColor'/><text x='185' y='105' font-size='12'>2 coins</text></svg>
questions.image_description: Two pencils at 4 coins each and one eraser at 2 coins
questions.solution: Two pencils cost 2 times 4 = 8 coins. Add 2 coins for the eraser: 8 + 2 = 10 coins.

questions.question_text: The figure shows a plus-shape made of 5 equal squares. How many corners (vertices) does its outline have?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 180 180' font-family='sans-serif'><polygon points='60,20 120,20 120,60 160,60 160,120 120,120 120,160 60,160 60,120 20,120 20,60 60,60' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/></svg>
questions.solution: The plus-shape outline has 12 corners where the edges change direction.
